title: Simandou and Australian Supply Expansion Promise a Boost to Capesize Tonnage, but Iron Ore Prices Face China Headwinds
section_path: Simandou and Australian Supply Expansion Promise a Boost to Capesize Tonnage, but Iron Ore Prices Face China Headwinds
section_path_text: Simandou and Australian Supply Expansion Promise a Boost to Capesize Tonnage, but Iron Ore Prices Face China Headwinds
summary:
Main
Image reference: unnamed.png | assets/2025-11-12_simandou-and-australian-supply-expansion-promise-a-boost-to-capesize-tonnage-but_img_unnamed_3ca440c5d78b.png
Global iron ore supply is gaining solid momentum, with total seaborne shipments climbing toward 150 million tonnes in recent months, up from around 120 million tonnes earlier in 2024. Western Australia remains the dominant contributor, sustaining high and stable export levels, while Brazil and West Africa have stepped in aggressively, showing marked increases since mid-2024 as new mining capacity and logistics improvements come online. Image reference: unnamed (1).png | assets/2025-11-12_simandou-and-australian-supply-expansion-promise-a-boost-to-capesize-tonnage-but_img_unnamed28129_a01690660559.png
Australia, Brazil, and West Africa Lead the Charge
In the Pilbara, BHP is doubling down on growth, pledging A$1.4 billion to enhance Port Hedland infrastructure and sustain export capacity near 305 million tonnes per annum (Mtpa).
